title: Ensure route table changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure route table changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Routing tables are used to route network traffic between subnets and to network gateways. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to route tables.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through the expected path and prevent any accidental or intentional modifications that may lead to uncontrolled network traffic. An alarm should be triggered every time an AWS API call is performed to create, replace, delete, or disassociate a route table.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE Internal Only - General • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{($.eventSource = ec2.amazonaws.com) && ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }" 4. Note the <route-table-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <route-table-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<route-table-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for route table changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: Internal Only - General aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <route-table-changes-alarm> --metric-name <route-table-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79198-8 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Internal Only - General
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls / boto3 SDK Methods to Use:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to interact with CloudTrail.
   - Use `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to enumerate existing CloudTrail trails.
   - Use `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` to check if the trail is active and logging.
   - Use `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` to validate management event configurations.

2. **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
   - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<route-table-changes-metric>]'`
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - CloudTrail trails to validate multi-region logging.
   - Log groups associated with CloudTrail.
   - Metric filters established for changes to route tables.
   - CloudWatch alarms associated with monitoring metric filters.
   - SNS subscriptions for alarm notifications.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure `IsMultiRegionTrail` for at least one active CloudTrail trail is set to true.
   - Verify that the identified trails capture all management events.
   - Validate that metric filters include the required filter pattern for route table changes.
   - Ensure at least one CloudWatch alarm is associated with the filter.
   - Confirm there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic for the alarm.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - Pass: At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail is found with logging enabled, includes management events, has a metric filter for route table changes, has an associated alarm with a valid SNS topic, and that SNS topic has active subscriptions.
   - Fail: One or more of the above checks are not met.

6. **Example:** 
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to enumerate the trails. Check if any trails have `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to True and that `IsLogging` is True.
   - Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)` to validate the metric filters present for the specified log group. 

This automated approach allows for consistent and efficient verification of whether route table changes are being monitored correctly, following best practices as outlined by the CIS benchmark.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- The classification as Automated is mostly correct, as the checks can be performed using AWS APIs, boto3, and CLI commands. However, the assessment could benefit from a more comprehensive approach that includes AWS Config rules to continuously monitor changes to route tables, which would enhance the automation and compliance monitoring capabilities.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- The automation details are mostly accurate, but there are some areas for improvement:
  - The use of `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors` is appropriate for checking management events, but it should explicitly ensure that the event selectors include changes to route tables.
  - The CLI command `aws logs describe-metric-filters` should specify the correct filter pattern for route table changes, which is not detailed here.
  - The program name `aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_monitoring_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific to indicate the exact nature of the monitoring, such as including 'metric_filter' or 'alarm_configured'.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Missing Considerations:**
  - The assessment should ensure that the metric filter pattern specifically matches API calls related to route table changes, such as `CreateRoute`, `DeleteRoute`, `ReplaceRoute`, etc.
  - Consider using AWS Config rules to automatically evaluate the configuration of AWS resources and alert on non-compliance, which can complement the existing CloudTrail and CloudWatch setup.
  - Include validation of the SNS topic policy to ensure it allows the necessary permissions for CloudWatch to publish to it.

- **Alternative AWS API Methods:**
  - Consider using `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` directly to verify the existence and configuration of alarms, rather than relying solely on CLI commands.

- **Better Program Name Suggestion:**
  - `aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_metric_filter_alarm_configured`

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- While the approach is generally sound, the lack of specific details on metric filter patterns and the potential for using AWS Config reduces confidence in the completeness of the assessment.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Modify:
  - Enhance the automation details by specifying the exact metric filter pattern for route table changes.
  - Consider integrating AWS Config rules for continuous compliance monitoring.
  - Update the program name to be more descriptive of the specific checks being performed.
  - Ensure that SNS topic policies are verified for correct permissions.

This structured approach will ensure a more thorough and accurate compliance audit for monitoring route table changes in AWS.
